evals.description: B2B SaaS target — mines G2, Capterra, and Reddit
assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
platforms_mined array lists the platforms that were checked
reviews array contains entries with text, source, sentiment, and theme_tags fields
Each review has verbatim text — not paraphrased summaries
Each review has sentiment as one of 'positive', 'negative', 'neutral', or 'mixed'
theme_tags is a non-empty array of strings for each review
themes_summary object contains top themes ranked by frequency with representative quotes
language_patterns array contains exact phrases customers use (if focus_themes includes language_patterns)
Output contains no recommendations — themes describe what was found, not what to do about it

evals.description: With competitor comparison — returns separate target and competitor data
assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
target_reviews and competitor_reviews arrays are both present
competitor_comparison object is present with at least avg_rating and top_praise and top_complaint fields for each
switching_reasons array captures reasons people switch between the two products (if found)

evals.description: Fewer than 10 reviews found — still returns valid structure with limitation noted
assertions: Returns valid JSON — does not fail when review count is low
total_reviews_found accurately reflects how many reviews were actually found
reviews array may be empty or have fewer than 10 entries
Output does not fabricate reviews or inflate counts
